ocr_text:
Is Gold Reflecting Commodity Costs?
article on link between record gold and silver prices and effects on commodity costs; says some commodity experts believe recent advance in precious metals are merely tail of inflationary forces unleashed by past rises in raw-materials costs; says others believe rise in metals means future increases of raw materials since price of precious metals is inverse gauge of value of paper money; notes bankers fear potentially disastrous effects that rising metal prices could mean in form of inflation; graph shows link between commodities and gold, Jan '72-Feb '74 (L)

Earnings of Ford Tumbled 76% in Final '73 Quarter
Ford Motor Co repts its earnings for '73 4th qr were $57-million, compared with $240-million in '72 corresponding period; repts net income for '73 was record $907-million, compared wtih $870-million in '72; graph compares Ford's total revenue and net income from '64 to 73

Auto Boom Amid Signs Of Revolt
article on major problems confronting auto indus in '74 notes that there is possibility of consumer revolt against 'burdensome' auto-safety equipment

LEADING INDEXES UP 1.7% IN MARCH;Dent Says Gain Indicates Economy Will Improve—Some Are Doubtful
Potlatch Corp on April 26 approves investments of more than $100-million for 140,000-ton pulp and paperboard mill in south-eastern Arkansas

Junta's Harsh Economic Measures Stagger Chileans
article, discussing Chile's mil junta's program for econ stability, notes that workers will bear brunt of efforts to increase indus production; workweek has already been lengthened from 44 hrs to 48 hrs; workers not inspired by patriotic calls for 'natl reconstruction' are cowed by possibility of losing their jobs; so-called 'extremists' have been dismissed at hundreds of factories, and all union activity has been prohibited; unemployment looms as critical problem; jobless rate in greater Santiago area under Allende was lowered from 8% to less than 4% by creation of thousands of fictional posts in Govt enterprises and agencies

WINE TALK
Article on Simi Winery head winemaker Mary Ann Graf, who is probably only woman in US to hold that title; discusses functions of head winemaker; co owner Russell Green comments on Grafs ability; Graf illus (L)

Measles Outbreak Strikes At Teen‐Agers in Bergen
NJ Acting Health Comr Dr William J Dougherty repts 252 new cases of measles, 169 more than usual, from Sept '73 through Mar '74 in NJ; says outbreak is spreading rapidly and affecting teenagers, particularly in Bergen County; cases reptd in various areas noted; Dr Thomas M Drew, health dept epidemiologist, holds disease becomes more severe as affected person gets older; health dept reptdly has distributed over 100,000 cases of vaccine since Sept; Paterson Mayor Thomas Rooney notes disease is on wane in city (M)

Simon a Firm Believer in Free‐Enterprise, Free‐Market System With a Minimal Government Role
Treas Sec designate William E Simon is convinced believer in free-mkt system with govt interference kept to minimum; his philosophy discussed (L)

Low Italian Dudgeon
R Baker humorous article on Columbus Day

MAYOR ACTS TO PAY TOWN FOR HER LIFE
Mrs C Robinson, Mayor of Metter, Ga, says she ran for office to repay town's citizens for saving her life; Robinson, first woman Mayor in town's history, underwent surgery for terminal cancer 5 yrs ago; drs recently told Robinson, who has been Mayor for almost 1 yr, that her cancer had returned; she has been undergoing chemotherapy treatments for 8 wks, and drs tell her that they will know in another mo whether treatments have been successful
GROWING DEFICITS EXPECTED IN WAKE OF OIL‐PRICE RISE

GROWING DEFICITS EXPECTED IN WAKE OF OIL‐PRICE RISE
Accelerating recessionary forces coupled with further painful increases in cost of living are foreseen Dec 24 in W Eur and in other industrialized and developing countries in wake of doubling of price of crude oil by major Persian Gulf exporting states; EEC comm estimates prolonged oil crisis could double unemployment and plunge Eur into deep recession; situation in specific countries discussed
